Mergen ADE

Purpose
- Native Rust desktop app for managing multiple terminal sessions in a tiled workspace.
- Windows-first runtime and release flow, with Windows portable ZIP assets and signed/notarized macOS ARM64 DMG assets in CI.
- Not an IDE: no built-in editor, LSP, debugger UI, accounts, or telemetry.

Tech Stack
- Rust 2021
- eframe/egui for the native UI
- portable-pty for PTY/session management
- tattoy-wezterm-term + tattoy-wezterm-surface for terminal emulation/render data
- TOML config stored in user-local app data via directories::ProjectDirs

Product Surface
- Multi-project terminal workspace with embedded tiled terminal panes.
- Left activity rail switches between Project Explorer and Terminal Manager.
- Project Explorer includes project picker, directory tree/index, search, and source control view.
- Terminal Manager groups terminals by project and by kind (`foreground` / `background`).
- Per-project saved messages/snippets can be stored and reused from the UI.
- Main terminal visibility can switch between global view and selected-project view.
- Auto-tiling can operate on all visible terminals or only the selected project's terminals.
- Terminal headers and manager rows surface shared git status badges.

Terminal Features
- PTY-backed interactive shells with platform-aware defaults.
- Terminal snapshots include styled runs/cells, cursor shape/state, and scrollback history.
- Selection/copy logic is custom and tested for wrapped lines, wide cells, and empty rows.
- `Ctrl+C` copies when a selection exists; otherwise it sends interrupt immediately.
- Secondary click pastes immediately when no selection exists, or opens copy behavior when one does.
- Child-process shutdown is handled explicitly; Windows code includes job/process-tree cleanup paths.
- Terminal titles are sanitized, whitespace-compacted, and truncated for chrome display.

Persisted Config
- Config is stored as TOML in user-local app data via `directories::ProjectDirs`.
- Persisted project records include `id`, `name`, `path`, and `saved_messages`.
- Persisted UI state includes selected project, panel visibility, expanded/collapsed state, left sidebar tab, project filter mode, auto-tile scope, and main visibility mode.
- Persisted shell state stores only the default shell and normalizes it for the current platform during load.
- Live terminal sessions and scrollback are intentionally not restored.

Repo Map
- src/main.rs: app entrypoint and native window startup
- src/app.rs: main UI composition and app state orchestration
- src/terminal.rs: PTY runtime, terminal snapshots, child process lifecycle
- src/layout.rs: terminal tiling/grid calculation
- src/title.rs: terminal title normalization/truncation
- src/config.rs: config path resolution and load/save logic
- src/models.rs: persisted config and shared enums/types
- scripts/build-release.ps1: supported Windows release build path
- scripts/package-macos-release.sh: local/CI macOS packaging helper
- .github/workflows/release.yml: tagged release pipeline
- KNOWN_ISSUES.md: append-only issue history and regression context

Architecture Notes
- UI state lives primarily in src/app.rs and drives panels, project selection, and visible terminals.
- Terminal sessions are created off the UI thread and communicate through channels/events.
- Terminal rendering uses snapshot-style data structures instead of embedding a full external terminal widget.
- Persisted config is intentionally small: project list, shell preference, saved messages, and UI state.
- Live sessions and scrollback are runtime-only and are not persisted.
- Source control refresh runs through a shared worker/scheduler, not ad hoc UI-thread fetches.
- Directory indexing is snapshot-based and asynchronous, keyed by project/generation.
- The main window defaults to a large native viewport and does not persist window geometry.

Important Behaviors
- Windows is the primary target; PowerShell/CMD are the supported shells there.
- Non-Windows currently normalizes to zsh.
- The repo keeps a lightweight, native-first design; avoid heavy dependencies and browser-style abstractions.
- UI controls should stay visually light and icon-first unless emphasis is required.
- Keep KNOWN_ISSUES.md updated when diagnosing or fixing recurring problems.
- Config migrations should preserve existing user state where possible; app.rs includes recovery/merge logic.
- Source control auto-refresh is lightweight status polling; fetch remains a more deliberate/manual path.

Release And Packaging
- Official tagged releases publish two assets together: Windows portable ZIP and macOS ARM64 DMG.
- The release workflow is in `.github/workflows/release.yml` and only publishes when both Windows and macOS jobs succeed.
- Windows packaging produces `mergen-ade-<tag>-windows-x64-portable.zip` containing `mergen-ade.exe`.
- macOS packaging script builds a minimal `.app`, generates an `.icns` from `logo.png`, places `/Applications` symlink in the DMG, and outputs `mergen-ade-<tag>-macos-arm64.dmg`.
- Official macOS DMGs are signed with a Developer ID Application certificate, submitted with `notarytool`, then stapled and validated.
- On notarization failure, CI uploads diagnostics from `release-staging/macos/notary-submit.json` and `notary-detail.json`.
- The local macOS packaging script can still create a DMG without signing when Apple credentials are absent.
- The CI release is intentionally all-or-nothing: a broken macOS notarization path should block the tagged release instead of silently shipping Windows-only assets.

Safe Starting Points For Changes
- Layout issues: start in src/layout.rs, then verify callers in src/app.rs
- Terminal lifecycle, IO, resize, selection, or child exit behavior: start in src/terminal.rs
- Title formatting or truncation: start in src/title.rs
- Config persistence or migration behavior: start in src/config.rs and src/models.rs
- Sidebar/panel/workspace UI behavior: start in src/app.rs
- Source control badges, branch/ahead-behind parsing, refresh cadence: start in src/app.rs around source control snapshot/worker code
- Directory tree/search/index behavior: start in src/app.rs around directory snapshot/index code
- Release asset or DMG behavior: start in `.github/workflows/release.yml` and `scripts/package-macos-release.sh`

Build And Test
- cargo build --release
- cargo run --release
- cargo test
- cargo fmt
- Windows release binary: cargo build --release --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
- If cargo is missing in PowerShell: %USERPROFILE%\\.cargo\\bin\\cargo.exe <command>

Validation Expectations
- Add or update inline unit tests in the affected module when changing logic.
- At minimum, run cargo test after feature or bug-fix work.
- Run cargo fmt before finishing edits.
- For release-path changes, also inspect `scripts/__tests__/build-release.tests.ps1` and the macOS packaging script.

Constraints
- Do not commit target/ or generated executables.
- Do not write local paths, signing secrets, or runtime app-data artifacts into tracked files.
- Preserve existing low-memory/native-first approach.
- Keep commits scoped and avoid unrelated refactors.
- Do not weaken the macOS release guarantees by allowing official tags to publish without the promised DMG.

Useful Context For Agents
- Read AGENTS.md for repo-specific workflow constraints and subagent expectations.
- Read README.md for user-facing product and release context.
- Read KNOWN_ISSUES.md before large debugging work or when touching fragile behavior.
- If working on release reliability, read the macOS-related entries in KNOWN_ISSUES.md before changing the workflow.
